title: GPU Instancing
rules: separate instance preparation from draw submission
procedure: Update instance data in controlled batches.

primary_when: request is about reducing draw calls
request involves indirect draw or per-instance data
must_not_own_when: request is about CPU scheduling, patching, or neural training

solves: rendering large repeated object sets with low CPU draw overhead
feeding per-instance transforms and attributes into shaders through GPU buffers
connecting GPU visibility or LOD output to indirect draw submission
does_not_solve: compute-stage architecture before instance data exists

core_principles: Instance data layout is shared by CPU, compute kernels, and render shaders.
Indirect draw is strongest when instance count stays GPU-owned.
Visible-index indirection decouples canonical instance data from culled draw order.
Draw bounds are part of correctness and performance.
Batch by compatible mesh, material, shader keywords, and buffer layout.
Bind safe buffers or skip draw when real data is unavailable.
constraints: Do not draw indirect without valid instance data, visible indices, draw args, mesh, material, and bounds.
Do not rely on CPU-side per-instance loops for a GPU-driven instancing path.
Do not let shader buffer names drift from material binding code.
Do not read visible counts back each frame just to issue a draw.
Do not use overly small bounds that clip valid instances.
decision_rules: Use gpu-instancing when the task centers on repeated object rendering, indirect draw, visible index buffers, or per-instance shader data.
Attach gpu-compute when instance data or visibility is generated by compute kernels.
Attach gpu-pipeline-architecture when instancing is the final stage of a broader GPU pipeline.
Use visible index buffers when culling or LOD compacts a subset of instances.
Use per-layer or per-material render entries when render state differs.
Bind dummy buffers or skip draw to avoid graphics API warnings before real buffers exist.

preferred_patterns: canonical InstanceData buffer plus VisibleIndices indirection
GPU-built DrawArgs buffer from append count
material property block or material buffer binding kept in one render path
per-batch bounds derived from surface, chunk, or layer extents
safe fallback mesh/material for debug mode
explicit render entries for mesh/material/layer compatibility groups
avoid_patterns: CPU readback of visible count as routine draw setup
shared material state mutated unpredictably across batches
shader code assuming buffers are always bound
single global bounds for unrelated chunks when tighter bounds are available

recipes.name: Render a visible-index indirect instance set
recipes.when: Use when compute has produced canonical instances and a compact visible index buffer.
steps: Bind the InstanceData buffer and VisibleIndices buffer to the material or property block.
Copy the visible append count into a count buffer.
Build draw args using mesh index count, start index, base vertex, and visible count.
Keep CPU out of per-instance submission.

recipes.name: Create a safe debug instancing path
recipes.when: Use when validating GPU data before final art assets or materials are ready.
steps: Provide a fallback mesh with predictable bounds and normals.
Provide a simple instanced shader that reads the production instance layout.
Bind dummy buffers before real buffers exist or skip drawing cleanly.
Color batches by layer or debug state when useful.
Keep the debug path structurally equivalent to the release draw path.

recipes.name: Batch instances by render compatibility
recipes.when: Use when multiple layers, prefabs, or materials share one GPU pipeline.
steps: Group render entries by mesh, material, shader layout, and layer settings.
Keep each group's instance and visible index buffers compatible with the shader.
Build draw args per group when visible counts differ.
Use per-group bounds for culling correctness.
Avoid mutating shared material state without explicit property blocks or binding policy.

pitfalls: Forgetting that DrawArgs instance count must match visible instances, not total candidates.
Using visible index indirection in shader but binding the wrong buffer.
Letting material instancing or shader keywords differ inside one batch.
Submitting indirect draws with null buffers and causing API warnings.
Using huge bounds that erase renderer-side culling benefits.
Rebuilding draw args on CPU when the GPU already owns the visible count.
review_checklist: Is per-instance layout explicit and shared with shader code?
Are visible indices used correctly when culling or LOD is active?
Is DrawArgs layout and update policy clear?
Are mesh, material, shader keywords, and buffers compatible per draw?
Are bounds valid for all possible instances in the batch?
Does the render path avoid per-instance CPU submission?
Are missing buffers handled without graphics API warnings?

references.title: Indirect Rendering
references.interpretation: Use this first for indirect draw args, visible index indirection, and draw submission policy.
references.why: It defines the practical rendering handoff for GPU-produced instance sets.

references.title: GPU Buffer Contracts
references.interpretation: Use to keep instance and visible-index buffers consistent across CPU, compute, and shader code.
references.why: Instancing correctness depends on stable buffer layout.

references.title: GPU Pipeline Architecture
references.interpretation: Use when instancing is one stage of a larger GPU-driven pipeline.
references.why: It connects render submission to upstream compute stage products.
